books.title: A Confession
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.summary: A brief meditation on depression, philosophy, religion, and the meaning of life.

books.title: The Land That Time Forgot
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: Stranded U-boat survivors and their rescuers contend with dangerous beasts and prehistoric people in a strange lost world.

books.title: My Reminiscences
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.summary: Autobiographical sketches from the Bengali writer, poet, and artist Rabindranath Tagore.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: Green Forest Stories
books.author: Thornton W. Burgess
books.summary: A collection of four animal tales featuring the denizens of the Green Forest.

books.title: Nonsense Books
books.author: Edward Lear
books.summary: A collection of literary nonsense in the forms of limericks, songs, and short stories.

books.title: The Cherry Orchard
books.author: Anton Chekhov
books.summary: A heavily indebted landowner and her family return to Russia in an attempt to save her estate from being auctioned off.

books.title: The Windfairies
books.author: Mary De Morgan
books.summary: The third collection of fairy tales by Mary De Morgan.

books.title: Beric the Briton
books.author: G. A. Henty
books.summary: A young Briton, learned in Roman ways, becomes their chief adversary.

books.title: The House on the Borderland
books.author: William Hope Hodgson
books.summary: An account of the terrifying metaphysical haunting of a recluse’s secluded home.

books.title: Essays
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: A collection of Henry David Thoreau’s essays.

books.title: Strong Poison
books.author: Dorothy L. Sayers
books.summary: A gentleman detective works to exonerate a woman accused of poisoning her ex-lover.

books.title: The Humbugs of the World
books.author: P. T. Barnum
books.summary: American archetype and cultural founding father P. T. Barnum shares a collection of anecdotes that he deems “humbugs”—fodder for the gullible, deserving of skepticism—with the goal of making his fellow citizens a little wiser and more skeptical.

books.title: The Religion of Nature Delineated
books.author: William Wollaston
books.summary: Wollaston attempts to derive ethics and religion by using rational thought and observation of the natural world, without resorting to revelation or received religion.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A collection of poetry by Scottish author, poet, and Christian minister George MacDonald, exploring themes of faith, nature, redemption, and the human soul.

books.title: Jack Keefe Stories
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A boastful, nearly illiterate major league pitcher writes a series of letters home detailing the goings-on in his life and career.

books.title: The Absolute at Large
books.author: Karel Čapek
books.summary: An inventor builds an atomic engine which converts matter into energy, but has the unfortunate side-effect of liberating divinity into the world.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Fritz Leiber
books.summary: A collection of Fritz Leiber short stories and novellas ordered by date of publication.

books.title: The City of God
books.author: Augustine of Hippo
books.summary: Bishop Augustine of Hippo provides a defense of the Christian faith against its pagan critics, as well as explaining God’s work and purposes in world history, from beginning to end.

books.title: A Negro Explorer at the North Pole
books.author: Matthew Henson
books.summary: The self-told story of the black man who accompanied Robert Peary during the first trip to the North Pole.
